D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Grue Languages — Blue and Green Share a Word

many languages (Welsh "glas", Vietnamese "xanh", Tarahumara "siyóname") cover blue and green with one term — "grue"; the split into separate words is the Berlin-Kay stage V transition

In Stage IV Berlin-Kay languages, "grue" spans the entire blue-green range. Adding a separate "blue" term (Stage V) requires a cultural reason — usually contact with another language or the introduction of synthetic indigo. Some languages still lack the split (Tarahumara, Pirahã).

Toggle Welsh vs English. Welsh "glas" covers grass and sky together.
